plan_metadata.name: Sample Teams center Dashboard Local Debug

steps.description: Click the "Search" input field in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Custom Engine Agent (4)" option in the "Platform capability" dropdown menu within the Samples tab of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Tab" dropdown filter in the "Samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code to open its filtering options.

Click the "Create" button for the "Team Central Dashboard" sample in the Samples tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Add ${{var:app_name:team-center-dashboard}} in to env.
Click the "Reload this page" button in the Chrome browser toolbar to refresh the Microsoft Teams web app tab.
Type 'https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab' into the Chrome browser's address bar on a new tab displaying the Google homepage.
Press Enter to select the highlighted URL (https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab) in the Chrome address bar on a new tab page.
Click the "Advanced" button on the Chrome privacy error page at https://localhost:53000, indicated by a bordered button below the warning message.
Click the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link on the Chrome privacy error page to bypass the security warning and continue to the localhost site.
Click the "Microsoft Teams Tab" browser tab at the top left of the Google Chrome window to activate it.
Click the red plus ("+") icon in the top-right corner of the "team-central-dashboardlocal" panel within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Reload" option in the context menu of a Microsoft Teams web app tab within Google Chrome.
Click the "Allow" button in the Microsoft Teams browser permission popup to grant access for connecting to devices on your local network.
Click on the "Password" input field in the Microsoft login page to focus and prepare for password entry.
Type ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the password input field on the Microsoft login page for account.
Press Enter key while focused on the password input field on the Microsoft login page (login.microsoftonline.com) to submit the password and attempt sign-in.
Click the "Return to the application without granting consent" link on the Microsoft login approval page.
